title: Backend API and Service Layer
summary: Implement .NET Web API with timer session management endpoints and business logic service layer.
businessValue: As a developer, I want a well-organized backend API so that the frontend can manage timer sessions reliably and persist user data.
outOfScope: Advanced business rules beyond V1 scope

stories.title: Create Timer Session with Mode and Task
stories.userValue: As an API consumer, I want to create a new timer session with a mode and task so that the frontend can initiate a Pomodoro session

acceptanceCriteria.given: a CreateTimerSession request with mode=Focus and task='Complete report'
acceptanceCriteria.then: a TimerSession is created with id, mode=Focus, duration=1500 seconds, task='Complete report', and createdAt timestamp; HTTP 201 is returned

acceptanceCriteria.given: a CreateTimerSession request with mode=Break and empty task
acceptanceCriteria.then: a TimerSession is created with duration=300 seconds and task is empty string

acceptanceCriteria.given: a session has been created
acceptanceCriteria.when: GetTimerSession is called with the new session id
acceptanceCriteria.then: the complete session object is returned

definitionOfDone: CreateTimerSession endpoint implemented in controller
CreateTimerSessionHandler created with business logic
CreateTimerSessionRequest and CreateTimerSessionResponse DTOs defined
TimerSession entity persisted to database
HTTP 201 response with session object returned
Unit tests for handler validation logic
Integration test for endpoint-to-database flow

stories.title: Retrieve Timer Session by ID
stories.userValue: As an API consumer, I want to retrieve a timer session by ID so that the frontend can fetch the current session state

acceptanceCriteria.given: a timer session with id=session-123 exists in the database
acceptanceCriteria.when: GetTimerSession endpoint is called with id=session-123
acceptanceCriteria.then: HTTP 200 is returned with the session object including mode, remainingTime, task, and state

acceptanceCriteria.given: no timer session exists with id=not-found-999
acceptanceCriteria.when: GetTimerSession endpoint is called with id=not-found-999
acceptanceCriteria.then: HTTP 404 is returned with error message

definitionOfDone: GetTimerSession endpoint implemented
GetTimerSessionHandler retrieves from database by ID
Returns complete session object with all fields
Proper HTTP status codes (200, 404)
Unit tests for success and not-found cases
Integration test for database retrieval

stories.title: Pause and Resume Timer Session
stories.userValue: As an API consumer, I want to pause and resume a timer session so that the frontend can control the timer workflow

acceptanceCriteria.given: a timer session with state=Running and remainingTime=1200
acceptanceCriteria.then: the session state becomes Paused and remainingTime=1200 is preserved

acceptanceCriteria.given: a timer session with state=Paused and remainingTime=1200
acceptanceCriteria.then: the session state becomes Running

acceptanceCriteria.given: a timer session with state=Paused
acceptanceCriteria.then: HTTP 400 is returned with validation error

definitionOfDone: UpdateTimerSession endpoint handles Pause and Resume actions
UpdateTimerSessionHandler validates state transitions
State change persisted to database
remainingTime preserved during pause
HTTP 200 response with updated session
Unit tests for state transition validation
Integration tests for pause/resume workflow

stories.title: Reset Timer to Mode Default Duration
stories.userValue: As an API consumer, I want to reset a timer session to its mode's default duration so that the user can restart the timer

acceptanceCriteria.given: a timer session with mode=Focus and remainingTime=1000
acceptanceCriteria.then: remainingTime is reset to 1500 (25 minutes) and state becomes NotStarted

acceptanceCriteria.given: a timer session with mode=Break and remainingTime=100
acceptanceCriteria.then: remainingTime is reset to 300 (5 minutes) and state becomes NotStarted

definitionOfDone: UpdateTimerSession endpoint handles Reset action
Business logic applies mode-specific default durations
remainingTime reset and state set to NotStarted
Changes persisted to database
HTTP 200 response with updated session
Unit tests for reset logic
Integration test for reset workflow

stories.title: Switch Timer Mode with Automatic Reset
stories.userValue: As an API consumer, I want to switch timer modes with automatic duration adjustment so that the user can follow the Pomodoro workflow

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Switch from Focus to Break mode
acceptanceCriteria.given: a timer session with mode=Focus, state=Running, and remainingTime=800
acceptanceCriteria.then: mode becomes Break, remainingTime resets to 300, state becomes NotStarted, and HTTP 200 is returned

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Switch from Break to Focus mode
acceptanceCriteria.given: a timer session with mode=Break and remainingTime=150
acceptanceCriteria.then: mode becomes Focus and remainingTime resets to 1500

definitionOfDone: UpdateTimerSession endpoint handles mode switching
Business logic applies correct duration based on target mode
State resets to NotStarted on mode change
previousMode state optionally tracked for UI purposes
HTTP 200 response with updated session
Unit tests for mode switch logic
Integration test for mode switching workflow

stories.title: Update Task Before Timer Starts
stories.userValue: As an API consumer, I want to update the task before the timer starts so that the user can change their focus objective

acceptanceCriteria.given: a timer session with state=NotStarted and task='Old task'
acceptanceCriteria.when: UpdateTimerSession is called with task='New task'
acceptanceCriteria.then: the session task is updated to 'New task' and HTTP 200 is returned

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Cannot update task on running session
acceptanceCriteria.given: a timer session with state=Running and task='Current task'
acceptanceCriteria.when: UpdateTimerSession is called with task='Different task'
acceptanceCriteria.then: HTTP 400 is returned with error message 'Cannot update task while timer is running'

acceptanceCriteria.given: a timer session with state=Paused and task='Some task'
acceptanceCriteria.then: the session task is cleared and HTTP 200 is returned

definitionOfDone: UpdateTimerSession endpoint handles task update
Business logic validates session state before allowing task update
Task update rejected if session is Running
Empty string accepted to clear task
Task field max length 200 characters validated
HTTP 200 on success, 400 on validation failure
Unit tests for task update validation
Integration test for task update workflow

stories.title: Complete Timer Session
stories.userValue: As an API consumer, I want to mark a timer session as complete so that the backend can track session lifecycle

acceptanceCriteria.given: a timer session with state=Running and remainingTime=600
acceptanceCriteria.then: the session state becomes Completed and completedAt timestamp is set to current time

acceptanceCriteria.given: a timer session with state=Paused
acceptanceCriteria.then: the session state becomes Completed regardless of remaining time

acceptanceCriteria.given: a timer session
acceptanceCriteria.then: HTTP 200 is returned with the completed session object including completedAt

definitionOfDone: CompleteTimerSession endpoint implemented
CompleteTimerSessionHandler updates session state to Completed
completedAt timestamp recorded as current UTC time
Session changes persisted to database
HTTP 200 response with completed session
Unit tests for completion logic
Integration test for completion workflow

stories.title: Define TimerSession and Task Data Models
stories.userValue: As a developer, I want well-defined TimerSession and Task entities so that the backend can consistently store and retrieve data

acceptanceCriteria.given: the TimerSession entity is reviewed
acceptanceCriteria.then: it contains: id (Guid), mode (enum: Focus/Break), state (enum: NotStarted/Running/Paused/Completed), remainingTime (int seconds), task (string), createdAt (DateTime), completedAt (DateTime?), lastUpdatedAt (DateTime)

acceptanceCriteria.given: the TimerSession task property is reviewed
acceptanceCriteria.then: it is nullable string with max length 200 characters

acceptanceCriteria.given: the Mode enum is reviewed
acceptanceCriteria.then: it supports exactly two values: Focus=0, Break=1

acceptanceCriteria.given: the State enum is reviewed
acceptanceCriteria.then: it supports exactly four values: NotStarted=0, Running=1, Paused=2, Completed=3

definitionOfDone: TimerSession.cs entity class created with all properties
Mode enum defined with Focus and Break
State enum defined with NotStarted, Running, Paused, Completed
Data annotations or fluent configuration for database constraints
Timestamps use DateTime (UTC)
remainingTime stored as integer (seconds)
Task property nullable with max length 200
Unit tests covering entity structure and validation

stories.title: Implement TimerSessionService Business Logic
stories.userValue: As a developer, I want a service layer with business logic so that controllers remain thin and logic is testable

acceptanceCriteria.given: TimerSessionService is called to create a session
acceptanceCriteria.then: the service sets remainingTime to 1500 (25 minutes); when mode=Break is specified, it sets remainingTime to 300 (5 minutes)

acceptanceCriteria.given: a timer session with state=NotStarted
acceptanceCriteria.when: the service is called to pause
acceptanceCriteria.then: it throws validation error because pause is not allowed from NotStarted

acceptanceCriteria.given: a timer session with state=Running
acceptanceCriteria.when: the service is called to update task
acceptanceCriteria.then: it rejects the update with error message 'Cannot update task while timer is running'

definitionOfDone: TimerSessionService class created
Methods: CreateSession, GetSession, UpdateSession, CompleteSession
Business logic for mode-to-duration mapping (Focus=1500, Break=300)
State transition validation (only allow valid transitions)
Task update restrictions enforced based on state
Service injected into controllers
Custom exceptions defined for business rule violations
Unit tests covering all business logic paths
Integration tests with repository/database

stories.title: Implement Consistent API Response Format
stories.userValue: As an API consumer, I want all endpoints to return a consistent response format so that the frontend can reliably parse responses

acceptanceCriteria.given: any successful API call
acceptanceCriteria.then: it contains: {success: true, data: {...}, statusCode: 200 (or appropriate success code)}

acceptanceCriteria.given: any failed API call (validation error, not found, etc.)
acceptanceCriteria.then: it contains: {success: false, error: {message, code, details}, statusCode: 400/404/500}

acceptanceCriteria.given: any response containing timestamps (createdAt, completedAt)
acceptanceCriteria.then: all timestamps are in ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2026-05-20T14:30:00Z)

definitionOfDone: ApiResponse<T> generic wrapper class created
ApiError class defined with message, code, and details
Global exception handler implemented to catch and format errors
All controllers wrap responses in ApiResponse
All timestamps serialized as ISO 8601 UTC
HTTP status codes set correctly for success and error scenarios
Unit tests for response formatting
Integration tests verifying end-to-end response format

stories.title: Setup Vertical Slice Architecture
stories.userValue: As a developer, I want the backend organized using vertical slice architecture so that features are cohesive and maintainable

acceptanceCriteria.given: the project structure is reviewed
acceptanceCriteria.then: it contains subdirectories: CreateTimerSession/, GetTimerSession/, UpdateTimerSession/, CompleteTimerSession/

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Each slice contains handler and models
acceptanceCriteria.given: the CreateTimerSession slice is reviewed
acceptanceCriteria.then: it contains: CreateTimerSessionHandler.cs, CreateTimerSessionRequest.cs, CreateTimerSessionResponse.cs

acceptanceCriteria.given: the TimerSessionsController is reviewed
acceptanceCriteria.then: it contains thin endpoints that delegate to appropriate service/handler classes

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Shared services are in Services folder
acceptanceCriteria.given: the project structure is reviewed
acceptanceCriteria.then: it contains TimerSessionService and other cross-cutting service logic

definitionOfDone: Project structure created: Controllers/, Features/, Services/, Models/, Data/
Features/TimerSessions/ subfolder with slice subdirectories created
Each feature slice contains handler, request, response classes
Controllers folder contains TimerSessionsController with thin endpoints
Services folder contains TimerSessionService
Models folder contains shared domain entities (TimerSession, Mode enum, State enum)
Data folder contains DbContext and migrations
Architecture documentation created explaining the pattern

stories.title: Implement Database Persistence Layer
stories.userValue: As a developer, I want timer sessions persisted to a database so that session data survives application restarts

acceptanceCriteria.given: a TimerSession object with mode, task, and initial state
acceptanceCriteria.when: the service saves it to the database
acceptanceCriteria.then: the session is persisted with an auto-generated id and timestamps, and can be queried by id

acceptanceCriteria.given: a previously saved session with id=session-123
acceptanceCriteria.when: queried by id through the service
acceptanceCriteria.then: the complete session object is retrieved with all fields intact

acceptanceCriteria.given: an existing session in the database
acceptanceCriteria.when: state or task is updated through the service
acceptanceCriteria.then: the changes are persisted and lastUpdatedAt is updated to current time

definitionOfDone: Entity Framework DbContext created and configured
TimerSession DbSet added to DbContext
Database migration created for TimerSession table schema
Connection string configured in appsettings.json
Repository pattern or direct DbContext usage implemented in service
CRUD operations tested against real database
Timestamps (createdAt, lastUpdatedAt) auto-set on create/update
Integration tests with test database or in-memory provider
